name: Nsc (Naca Counseling Subsidiary) - Atlanta, Ga
description_short: NACA is a non-profit mortgage lender offering character-based mortgages with no down payment, no closing costs, no mortgage insurance, and fixed rates below market—requiring membership qualification.
description_long:
Founded in 1988 by the Boston Hotel Workers Union, the Neighborhood Assistance Corporation of America (NACA) began as a housing advocacy organization and has evolved into a non-profit mortgage lender focused on closing the racial wealth disparity gap. The organization has served 3 million people and helped over 500,000 homeowners achieve affordable homeownership through character-based lending that doesn't rely on traditional credit scores.

NACA's flagship mortgage product eliminates traditional barriers to homeownership: zero down payment, zero closing costs or fees, zero mortgage insurance, and no credit score consideration. Their current fixed rates (as of April 2026) are 5.625% for 30-year mortgages, 5.125% for 20-year, and 5% for 15-year terms—positioned below market rates. To qualify, applicants must attend an "Achieve the Dream" event and complete the membership process. NACA also operates HUD-approved housing counseling services, which represent 30% of all HUD counseling nationally, and has issued $20 billion in mortgage commitments to date.

What distinguishes NACA is its explicit mission-driven approach to lending equity rather than profit maximization. The organization explicitly targets historically underserved communities, low-to-moderate income families, and people of color. They incorporate life circumstances and character assessment rather than credit scores into lending decisions, fundamentally rejecting traditional credit-based underwriting models.

The main caveat is that NACA mortgages are not accessible to everyone—applicants must qualify through their specific membership process and attend multi-day events, which creates geographic and logistical barriers. Additionally, while their rates are competitive, they are still subject to market conditions and require meeting NACA's specific qualification criteria beyond traditional lending parameters.
pros: Zero down payment required—eliminates largest barrier to homeownership for many consumers
No closing costs or fees—typically saves thousands of dollars at settlement
No mortgage insurance—removes ongoing PMI costs that standard low-down loans require
Credit score not considered—applicants assessed on character and circumstances instead
Below-market fixed rates—5.625% for 30-year mortgages competitive with market conditions
HUD-approved housing counseling included—30% of all national HUD counseling provided by NACA
Mission-driven non-profit—explicitly targets low-to-moderate income and underserved communities
cons: Membership qualification required—not all applicants approved; must attend multi-day 'Achieve the Dream' events
Geographic limitations—events held in select cities only (Little Rock, Milwaukee, Fort Myers, Hartford mentioned), limiting accessibility
Limited information on approval rates—website does not disclose what percentage of attendees qualify or conversion rates
Character-based assessment criteria not transparently defined—unclear exactly how 'character' and 'circumstances' are evaluated
No details on processing timelines—no disclosed timeframes for loan approval or closing
best_for: Low-to-moderate income first-time homebuyers with limited savings for down payment
People of color and historically underserved communities facing credit discrimination
Borrowers with poor or no credit history who cannot qualify for conventional mortgages
Homebuyers in regions with NACA 'Achieve the Dream' events who can attend multi-day qualification programs
diagnosis: NACA is best for low-to-moderate income homebuyers, particularly people of color and those with poor or no credit history, who can attend their multi-day qualification events and meet character-based lending criteria. The main caveat is that membership qualification is not guaranteed, geographic accessibility is limited to event locations, and the application process requires significant time commitment beyond standard mortgage applications.
services: Mortgage lending—no down payment mortgages with no closing costs
Character-based underwriting—credit score not considered in loan decisions
HUD-approved housing counseling—pre- and post-purchase homeownership education
Member portal—online access to file, document upload, and status tracking
Achieve the Dream events—multi-day in-person qualification and education sessions
Volunteer opportunities—NACtivist program for member engagement and activism
Predatory lending advocacy—consumer education and campaigns against predatory lenders
Partner networks—relationships with lenders, servicers, real estate agents, home inspectors
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
